461	
462	Stop-condition evaluation normally runs **between** `ddx try` invocations.
463	`signal` is the exception: the first signal cancels an in-flight attempt
464	cooperatively rather than waiting for the model to finish. The cancelled
465	attempt is mechanically disrupted, not a model no-progress failure, and ordinary
466	interrupt shutdown MUST NOT park the bead behind `execute-loop-retry-after`.
467	
468	### Execution Resource Cleanup
469	
470	DDx-created execution resources must have an owner, a liveness signal, a
471	retention policy, and a cleanup path. Missing ownership, missing liveness, or
472	unbounded accumulation is a correctness bug in the execution substrate.
473	
474	Layer 2 owns inline cleanup for one `ddx try` attempt. Before claim or worktree
475	creation it validates the required execution roots:
476	
477	- the temporary worktree root, currently `$TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt` unless
478	  overridden
479	- the durable evidence root, currently `.ddx/executions` during migration and
480	  `.ddx/runs` for new substrate records
481	- git worktree registration/removal for the project repository
482	
483	The validation checks writability, free bytes, and free inodes where the
484	platform exposes inode counts. If validation fails, `ddx try` runs one
485	immediate DDx-scoped cleanup pass, re-checks, and then either proceeds or
486	returns `resource_exhausted` without claiming the bead.
487	
488	`ddx work` and server-managed workers use the same cleanup manager before the
489	first claim and before later claims whenever temp free space drops below a soft
490	high-water threshold. If cleanup does not restore the temp roots above the hard
491	floor, the loop stops visibly with `resource_exhausted` and claims no more
492	beads.
493	
494	After an attempt starts, Layer 2 removes the isolated worktree when the result
495	has been merged, explicitly preserved, classified as no-changes/no-evidence, or
496	interrupted through the cooperative shutdown path. Failed setup must remove any
497	partial unregistered directory it created. A worktree may remain only when DDx
498	records an explicit preserve decision with evidence pointing at the retained
499	path or ref.
500	
501	DDx-owned cleanup scope includes execution worktrees, DDx-created test and e2e
502	scratch roots, generated test binaries, and run-state or liveness files. The
503	cleanup manager may delete only DDx-owned paths with ownership metadata or
504	known DDx prefixes, minimum age/mtime satisfied, and no live PID/session
505	liveness. It must preserve published evidence, registered active worktrees,
506	and anything outside DDx-owned roots.
507	
508	Layer 3 owns loop cleanup. `ddx work` runs cleanup:
509	
510	- once at startup, before the first queue claim
511	- before the next claim after any setup/finalization failure
512	- periodically while a long-lived poll worker remains active
513	- during graceful signal shutdown before exit
514	
515	Long-lived DDx processes also start a background cleanup worker. The background
516	worker runs occasionally with jitter and a project-level cleanup lock so multiple
517	workers do not all prune at once. It is conservative and DDx-scoped: it may
518	remove stale unregistered directories under DDx temp roots, registered DDx
519	worktrees whose attempt is terminal or whose liveness marker is stale, stale
520	heartbeat/liveness files for dead PIDs, and partial setup directories that were
521	never published as complete evidence. It must not remove preserved worktrees,
522	`refs/ddx/iterations/...`, complete `.ddx/runs/<id>` or
523	`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>` evidence, active worktrees with live
524	PID/session liveness, or non-DDx directories.
525	
526	Cleanup is observable but not noisy. Routine passes are trace/debug or worker
527	events. Passes that reclaim significant bytes or inodes emit an operator-visible
528	summary such as `cleanup: removed 37 stale ddx worktrees, freed 14210 inodes`.
529	Resource exhaustion after cleanup is a hard visible stop message and a layer-3
530	`resource_exhausted` disposition.
531	
532	Cleanup reporting includes scratch roots removed, bytes and inodes reclaimed,
533	preserved paths, and blocked warnings so operators can see why cleanup stopped
534	short.
535	
536	### Long-running default (`--poll-interval`)
537	
538	Per ddx-dc157075, the default `--poll-interval` for `ddx work` is **30s**.
539	With a positive poll interval the
540	worker stays alive across empty polls (`drained` becomes a transient
541	"running (idle)" substate, not a terminal exit). The loop exits only on
542	the conditions enumerated above plus `signal`, fatal config errors, or
543	the explicit operator opt-outs:
544	
545	- `--once` — process at most one ready bead, then exit.
546	- `--poll-interval=0` — legacy "drain-and-exit" semantics: when the
547	  queue empties, return immediately without polling.
548	
549	Server-managed workers spawned via `POST /api/workers/execute-loop`
550	inherit the same 30s default when the request omits `poll_interval`. The
551	worker record exposes a `substate` field set to `"idle"` while the loop
552	is sleeping between empty polls; it is cleared as soon as a candidate is
553	picked. Terminal worker states (`exited`, `failed`, `stopped`, `reaped`)
554	always clear `substate`.
555	
556	The structured `loop.end` event carries an `exit_reason` field
557	(`once_complete`, `explicit_poll_zero`, `sigint`, `sigterm`,
558	`fatal_config`, `resource_exhausted`; `providers_exhausted` is reserved for the
559	quota-pause work in ddx-aede917d).

680	## Requirements
681	
682	### Functional
683	
684	1. **One on-disk substrate** — all run records, regardless of layer,
685	   persist under `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/` with the common record shape and
686	   a discriminating `layer` field.
687	2. **Layer metadata** — every run record carries `layer`,
688	   `parent_run_id`, and the layer-appropriate extension subobject.
689	3. **Layer-1 invocation** — `ddx run` produces exactly one layer-1
690	   record per invocation; consumes upstream Fizeau execution contract
691	   per CONTRACT-003.
692	4. **Layer-2 attempt** — `ddx try <bead>` produces exactly one layer-2
693	   record, references its child layer-1 records, and finalizes the
694	   worktree as `merge` or `preserve`.
695	5. **Layer-3 drain** — `ddx work` produces exactly one layer-3 record
696	   per invocation, references its child layer-2 attempt ids, and reports
697	   one of the terminal dispositions (`drained`, `blocked`, `deferred`,
698	   `no_progress`, `signal`, `resource_exhausted`).
699	6. **Stop conditions** — `ddx work` evaluates the stop conditions
700	   between attempts and persists the evaluation log on the layer-3
701	   record.
702	7. **No-progress detection** — the `no_progress` condition is mechanical
703	   (no commit, no merged side-effect) and configurable via
704	   `.ddx/config.yaml` (default `N = 3`).
705	8. **Append-only history** — a new run never rewrites a previous record
706	   at any layer.
707	9. **Cross-layer query** — `ddx runs` lists records across all three
708	   layers with layer filtering; `ddx tries` and `ddx work workers`
709	   are layer-specific projections over the same substrate.
710	10. **Run detail inspection** — for any run id, callers can retrieve the
711	    common fields, the layer extension, attachments, and child run ids.
712	11. **Generated-artifact provenance** — runs that regenerate an artifact
713	    set `produces_artifact: <id>` on the run record; FEAT-007 reads this
714	    to populate `generated_by` graph edges.
715	12. **Migration from legacy paths** — readers tolerate
716	    `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` during a
717	    documented migration window and surface them as layer-1 / layer-2
718	    records; writers always target `.ddx/runs/`.
719	13. **Configuration** — substrate root, no-progress threshold, deferred
720	    budget, and retention settings are configurable in
721	    `.ddx/config.yaml`.
722	14. **Execution cleanup** — `ddx try` and `ddx work` remove stale DDx-owned
723	    execution worktrees, DDx-created test/e2e scratch roots, generated test
724	    binaries, and liveness files through inline, loop, and background cleanup
725	    paths without deleting preserved attempts or published evidence.
726	15. **Resource preflight** — `ddx try` validates writable execution roots and
727	    free bytes/inodes before claim; failed validation may trigger one cleanup
728	    retry, and `ddx work` / server-managed workers run cleanup before claim
729	    whenever temp free space drops below a soft high-water threshold. If the
730	    temp roots remain below the hard floor after cleanup, the loop returns
731	    `resource_exhausted` without claiming.
732	
733	Expected implementation tests include `TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs`,
734	`TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor`, and
735	`TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup`.
736	
737	### Non-Functional
738	
739	- **Determinism:** the persisted record reflects the exact prompt,

275	  metadata or registered worktree evidence
276	
277	### Resource preflight and loop-fatal handling
278	
279	Before `ddx try` claims a bead or creates a worktree, it checks the configured
280	temp worktree root and durable evidence root for writability, free bytes, and
281	free inodes when inode data is available. If preflight fails, `ddx try` runs one
282	immediate cleanup pass and repeats the check. If the check still fails, the
283	attempt returns `resource_exhausted` without claiming the bead.
284	
285	`ddx work` and server-managed workers perform the same cleanup pass before the
286	first claim and before any later claim when temp free space falls below a soft
287	high-water threshold. If the temp roots remain below the hard floor after that
288	cleanup, the loop records host exhaustion and stops claiming new beads.
289	
290	If resource exhaustion occurs after a claim or during worktree setup,
291	`ddx try` records whatever partial evidence is available, removes any partial
292	unregistered directory it created, releases the claim if the bead did not close,
293	and returns `resource_exhausted`.
294	
295	`ddx work` treats `resource_exhausted` as host infrastructure failure. It
296	records a layer-3 stop-condition entry, prints an operator-visible message such
297	as `resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop`, and stops claiming
298	new beads. It must not continue scanning the ready queue in the same process.
299	
300	Cleanup output is structured. Routine passes are trace/debug or worker events.
301	Passes that reclaim meaningful bytes or inodes emit an operator-visible summary
302	including counts. Failures include path, class, and whether the failure blocked
303	progress.
304	
305	Expected implementation tests include `TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs`,
306	`TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor`, and
307	`TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup`.
308	
309	## Drill-Down Query Model
310	
311	The read API is centered on `ddx runs`:
312	
313	- `ddx runs list --layer 1|2|3 --bead <id> --artifact <id>`
314	- `ddx runs show <run-id>`
315	- `ddx runs children <run-id>`
316	- `ddx runs parents <run-id>`
317	- `ddx runs log <run-id>`
318	- `ddx runs result <run-id>`
